approval_policy: A source status authorizes planning only. Download requires a separate user approval after dated snapshot, license evidence, attribution, and checksum review.

sources.approval_reason: Ready after official dump hashes and page-level attribution are captured.
sources.attribution_requirements: Retain project, snapshot, article URL or stable attribution route, and license in the corpus manifest.
sources.checksum_availability: official Wikimedia md5sums and sha1sums files
sources.extraction_method: stream BZ2 and iterative MediaWiki XML parsing; main namespace only
sources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 and GFDL; imported text may carry compatible additional attribution
pii_privacy_risks: revision text may contain transient PII
sources.redistribution_terms: Preserve source and license notices; comply with page-level imported-content notices.

sources.approval_reason: Ready with the exact Python 3.14.6 archive, PSF attribution, and example-code license metadata.
sources.attribution_requirements: Python Software Foundation, version, documentation URL, and license notice.
sources.checksum_availability: no official archive checksum observed; capture SHA-256 immediately after approved download
sources.extraction_method: ZIP member streaming with path allowlist and strict UTF-8 text decoding
sources.license: Python Software Foundation License Version 2; code examples additionally Zero-Clause BSD
pii_privacy_risks: author names and emails in acknowledgements
sources.redistribution_terms: Retain PSF copyright and license notices; identify separately licensed examples.

sources.approval_reason: Requires sentence and translation-chain attribution plus license completeness enforcement.

sources.checksum_availability: no stable official checksum; capture SHA-256 and export timestamp

sources.license: CC BY 2.0 FR per sentence export; some sentences also CC0
sources.redistribution_terms: Preserve sentence-level license and author attribution; do not include audio.

sources.approval_reason: Page-level public-domain and contributor-license filtering is not implemented.

contamination_risks: multiple editions of the same work
sources.extraction_method: stream XML; accept only pages with machine-verifiable rights templates
sources.license: work-specific public-domain or free-license status plus Wikimedia site terms
sources.redistribution_terms: Every work requires retained work-level rights metadata and jurisdiction review.

sources.approval_reason: Requires file-level prose/code license separation and pre-2010 code handling.
sources.attribution_requirements: Page title, URL, Mozilla Contributors, contributors list where reasonable, license, and modification notice.
sources.canonical_name: MDN Web Docs English content repository
sources.checksum_availability: Git commit SHA after pinning
sources.edition: 2026-07-13 candidate snapshot; exact commit SHA required before approval
sources.extraction_method: pinned Git tree; parse frontmatter and Markdown; separate prose and code licenses
sources.license: documentation generally CC BY-SA 2.5 or later; code samples CC0 or legacy MIT
sources.redistribution_terms: Preserve page attribution, modifications, and license distinctions for prose and code.

sources.approval_reason: Requires work-level public-domain checks for the training jurisdiction and header cleanup.
sources.attribution_requirements: Work title, author, ebook ID, rights statement, access date, and jurisdiction note.
sources.canonical_name: Project Gutenberg selected English public-domain texts
sources.checksum_availability: per-file SHA-256 must be captured; official checksum coverage varies
contamination_risks: multiple editions of the same work
sources.extraction_method: approved per-work manifest only; strip Gutenberg headers while retaining provenance
sources.license: mostly public domain in the United States with Project Gutenberg trademark/license conditions
sources.redistribution_terms: Per-work rights verification and Project Gutenberg trademark/license compliance are required.

sources.approval_reason: Requires revision-date license versioning, user attribution, and code/content separation.
sources.attribution_requirements: Site, post ID, author, post URL, revision date, and applicable CC BY-SA version.
sources.canonical_name: Selected Stack Exchange technical sites data dump
sources.checksum_availability: Internet Archive item checksums; verify each selected archive
sources.extraction_method: stream selected site XML; retain revision/license metadata; strip user profiles
sources.license: revision-date-dependent CC BY-SA 2.5, 3.0, or 4.0
sources.publisher: Stack Exchange, Inc. and individual contributors
sources.redistribution_terms: Revision-level license and attribution must be retained; share-alike implications require review.
